rulings.Case Title: JOANNE SIU ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 3, 2023 line 1. HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is OFF CALENDAR. Stipulation and order to stay of proceedings filed on February 7, 2023. Petitioner may re-notice after the stay is lifted. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TAL N. TAMIR ET AL VS. LEM HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Issue, Evidentiary And Monetary Sanctions Against Lem Holdings, Llc, Ciaran Harty And Patrick Harty For Spoliation And Suppression Of Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 3, 2023 line 2. PLAINTIFF TAL TAMIR, CELINE TAMIR Motion For Issue, Evidentiary And Monetary Sanctions Against Lem Holdings, Llc, Ciaran Harty And Patrick Harty For Spoliation And Suppression Of Evidence is GRANTED. The following facts are deemed established as issue sanctions: (1) DD failed to preserve evidence as required and, in fact, improperly destroyed / spoliated such evidence, (2) the evidence improperly destroyed and otherwise withheld including, but not limited to, text messages and emails with each other, agents/brokers for prospective purchasers of the home (135 Yukon Street, San Francisco, CA), and others concerning, among other things, defects with the construction of the Home, water/flooding damage to the property, disclosures made to others and not Plaintiffs, the subject real estate purchase transaction as well as DD's knowledge of adverse facts and information concerning the home which it concealed from Plaintiffs, and (3) the trier of fact shall draw an inference that those documents and this information would have been unfavorable to Defendants. Evidentiary sanctions granted and Defendants are barred from introducing any evidence or making any arguments in contravention of the foregoing issue sanctions. Monetary sanctions granted in the amount of $17,125 against Defendants jointly and severally to be paid within 30 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARK SMITH VS. JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss And Supporting Memorandum By Defendants Jones Memorial Homes, Inc. And Arlene Henry
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 3, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC, ARLENE HENRY Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss And Supporting Memorandum By Defendants Jones Memorial Homes, Inc. And Arlene Henry is DENIED, without prejudice. The motion was filed prematurely, prior to the entry of order striking the First Amended Complaint. rulings.Case Title: ZEN LOUIE LLC ET AL VS. PIER 39 LIMITED PARTNERSHIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 3, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT PIER 39 LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration is CONTINUED to June 14, 2023. A hearing concerning Plaintiffs inability to pay for arbitration is set for June 14, 2023. (Aronow v. Superior Court (2022) 76 Cal.App.5th 865.) Plaintiff to file an opening brief regarding insolvency and admissible evidence regarding ability/inability to pay by May 3, 2023. Opposition due May 24, 2023. Reply due May 31, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
